source: BEAM-101 clinical data, Casgevy (exagamglogene autotemcel), FDA 2024 Gene Editing Guidance
description: Expected effects for RNP/electroporation-delivered gene editing in ex vivo context. Cleanest toxicity profile — no viral or LNP vehicle effects. Conditioning regimen (busulfan myeloablation) dominates the safety profile.

expected_findings.rationale: Electroporation-induced cell stress. Expected in ex vivo manufacturing.

expected_findings.description: Residual Cas9 protein in cell product
expected_findings.severity_threshold: Above assay-specific threshold = release criteria concern
expected_findings.rationale: RNP Cas9 protein degrades within 24-48h. Monitored as quality attribute in cell product.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Selection of p53-null clones = oncogenic concern
expected_findings.rationale: DSB-induced DNA damage response. Nuclease editors only.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Balanced translocations or clonal abnormalities = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: NHEJ between DSB sites can produce translocations. Required by FDA 2024 guidance.

expected_findings.severity_threshold: Any clone >20% dominance = investigate for clonal selection
expected_findings.rationale: Expected polyclonal engraftment. Clonal dominance may indicate selective advantage from unintended editing.
Expected engraftment kinetics post-conditioning. BEAM-101: safety profile consistent with busulfan conditioning and autologous HSCT.

expected_findings.description: Anti-Cas9 immune response (minimal for RNP ex vivo)
expected_findings.rationale: Minimal for RNP delivery — Cas9 protein degrades rapidly in ex vivo product. Lower concern than in vivo delivery.

expected_findings.typical_magnitude: Hundreds per genome for rAPOBEC1-based CBEs
expected_findings.severity_threshold: SNVs in tumor suppressor genes = adverse
expected_findings.rationale: APOBEC deaminase activity on single-stranded DNA. Base editor-specific.
